name: Sheriff Chad Bianco
subject: We can win in California again

body:
| | | Kamala Harris is gearing up for the California Governor’s race.
---
| |
---
| |
---
| | Dear Peter,You may recognize me as Riverside County Sheriff. As Sheriff I am responsible for the County's five jail facilities, six court buildings, a civil bureau, the Coroner's Bureau, Public Administrator's Office, twelve patrol stations, seventeen contract cities, 4,200 dedicated employees and an operating budget of just over one billion dollars.It’s a lot of responsibility, but having the honor of keeping my home safe is the best job in the world.
---
| | Throughout my career in law enforcement, I have fought back against the pro-criminal policies coming out of Sacramento. We have held the line and kept Riverside County safe. Now, the threat that the Democrat leadership poses to our communities is graver than ever before.
---
| [](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/vwcvzWTeo5UfiAgvaspPodNsfdT0V3Pdgl3y76wluYC-5cO5TLL4MBnBoYs_EHFwxX-osOjbIhI7MH6LkyRDaP5a60uadAUVCeK9_1HrMz22z5tHDkH9ZULDdhtKgskLi3JmJJDLvgCqjhqpIQpzBdROrA9Ii9CNlqQ1dF0YlYVoU3iOR2Hda1EPpQMfJh2SBQ3i7c1ZSQ7bYZv2uVc4KLf-VTVJ5Z1ZziPssQrkjj1-0g2mnrirteDJMrrV0m1DoBMbbe5pzsrJOrhftYEDPG6mHWwhaEZq3ni03aonOYMpYgaaGnLLSQcn5Hum9VMVGmuSK9Dj7zgWkvHaGiIpJdDkfFle9ZS3cJKmI2ZqQ2cKTfXFp07mvrRCFwRn3zKS0_9ZmRnHbTeHjRXzn7W2Ex-R1ZkBnKvmKfXSiwgFI0yM-ROvrm_8cPsDcALMucFHUdEEGRetCJaKb6C26TC7SzGQJinG4Jk-FNtuYuJIRwPyJbSC_bwnjpRTktF_B7zd/vMSHoqNylSiWGCz-qu893yhLAvYsJVpy/7)
---
| | That’s because Kamala Harris is positioning herself to run for Governor of California. **[Today, I’m asking you to help me defeat radical Kamala Harris in the race for California Governor.](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/fNkhTUT4d7SMC1ZujODBUEcZfO2ti9JYk0maHNnv4x8neRq_xULX8QPeeQHJ52TOhgwFWxE0YqK5xJXfQtlB4fJ8a0GTwbu9JmdHg1hMrJ4HmatK3AH95En9nfyleQNZzCcY6wTjvlB_lnPKo9DIn6UDYXnUWnJuLnmCeTJVmz68GeBHdpCws4ZoZBLnfxUzEjqX6yLmxg-Q88V-dZFw0HVlmPI0onOHY_occcqXVxlBqXgVSfz0trn_yr3XsC79tk54b7kydKya9UalxqcKPEab81pJgpI_HYPUR8SAYtXpqoaN8zYY0FbGp9_k3J625zUxp1Ys_Pbb2-x_whQbQS4_6ha0Tx5_zkdynSrkxejLrrutzJ4nzOyYLprKYbReK2dEG05PYknh7lMsZ5XilFv6gwnZ8yildra6Hr9z9rkUhMiW3opEjGgAdj5UI3HcOwg7tqSXUdEhdYk3XxPqKbY-KgMm3qlECvM-7KzUil956zdYFuQJzv4a05gB4-4u/q9Otqd-SwljkQV55RhlzrprjlGkCpSec/7)** Californians are waking up to how disastrous these years of Democrat rule have been. There is no better way to highlight that failure than putting myself, a Sheriff who has spent decades defending law and order, up against Kamala Harris, who as Attorney General oversaw the severe decline in public safety that we are living with today.Kamala Harris, Karen Bass, Katie Porter, and Adam Schiff want us to forget the harm that Democrat rule has caused to California.But you know how these years have been:
---
* Democrats’ lack of preparation and water management allowed wildfires to ravage the state, while Karen Bass and Gavin Newsom were nowhere to be found.
* Despite Democrats spending hundreds of millions to “fix” it—crime, homelessness, and drug addiction skyrocketed.
* Democrats wasted billions of dollars on “high-speed rail” with nothing to show for it.

---
As they say: fool me once, shame on you... Fool me twice, shame on me.Well Californians ought to be ashamed.There’s rampant crime, homelessness, and drug addiction. The cost of living is unbearable...Californians are being taxed to death just to watch their hard-earned dollars go towards fighting against Trump’s common-sense policies like wildfire prevention, immigration reform, and water storage improvement.These California Democrats don’t care one bit that Americans are having a hard time. I’d go even further to say they _welcome_ the misery so they can keep folks down and dependent.And I wouldn’t blame you if you said, “Chad, I’m afraid California’s best days are behind us.”But listen, here’s something you need to remember...**_Republicans turned things around_ by electing Ronald Reagan as Governor of California.****_And we can do that again_**. **But there’s no way I can pull it off without you.****[That’s why I’m asking you to rush your $35 donation to my campaign today.](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/R90JBXw5WvSMoSibzu_cXy0kpyLG4liibeiCV-RVIYaObOoFbnasZ8DOPZqyMzK-eJRTyGChb8rWJYVv-n6yA7PIyvK6wv9a66vMh8C0e_J7PZfLAl2iUz-4uRy4x55G7eeQOYILDAT3fyfaDqPv8akP5a9h7wiuBDUARLX7382PifND5kEXQkzEBEsoqec2l8GWXYp0NWlxYcMpODRlatjcL0Tmrdu4lk-NsUpmQbUg_DTMvo-r41DKx6-lvCM9S9XfoLS6C6QS7Tlg90iyBfKYx0EJ1Ls8THWuu7HzujBtqYLvME3SMp7biCFc2UAwpBOIYvJNh5rsF31g0MuGXKEqEPJ2f9xo7c5x4cLZGcyE06rPF-WZu0U4FHmK-3yo4cumvJjO3vZZsabYaGzNiAQO6E95BsBZAYkdWUHgNM-INEiNkfIhQbnUvjiQHbmPmGsFxruId9df_c3kho2cFjiXjwzaZbM19tlSGq7H68vbXnJ_4nfU6GhneeSIWLyJ/rf_UsAqAOm9j4ugnQiOLsnraV3iQM-t3/7)** More and more everyday Americans are waking up to the harm that years of Democrat rule has caused them, and they are turning their backs on the party that betrayed them. A recent statewide poll showed Gov. Gavin Newsom's approval rating among likely voters at 44%. The number for the state legislature is even lower, with a 40% approval rating. Fewer than 20% of L.A. residents give Mayor Karen Bass high marks for fire response. Six in 10 voters said they felt the state was going in the wrong direction. The polling for Republicans has never been stronger in 20 years because of the dissatisfaction with the Democrats controlling their government.The time is right...But California is massive. We need to ignite a massive grassroots movement to compete across all 163,696 square miles and in all 58 counties. **[**When you rush your Bianco for California contribution of $35 or more...**](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/GKe42HuIMo29rVG-3Gj1CPYVSA_ppdvBJUsdE-LEBq-xYlv08yyZRIi2TugiK-6NITsusZIObeLClnEt0jvr5JTroQG4VdUtMB242pDiR6ZHsgumOLeQPPSTT58sWcDi4YWDKmihpLGvAda0Sm6A31B7y-edp2dG-0ovN0HgipGqCSi-uvl_N739qonmpj_ykNmxn6on0PVVKCxKziQnL09iG2nUIxJeIqv3Yy7YAqGvxxF5j8bylZ7ZyKxjG4Z4FxeoMaJ2QmOqjgF3QkfjG4HJ9ZuxNr1IykokhrvoblcPlMdu57GIh7iSmU_s7oNK6B9WcmQhKQNGO1NF9SENAbREZ5X7CvAScpwjI-eBo7FjB_mX4_vsrCv5HfqFB8_hkK3beCDmhjIOyMfitz-iKwC27brOTimp5RRgkwIoGk3WKiG12DI8Aj7UWRzxA0LAkM46CtipAddjqedqlOm7HmzZhxQzyY9sQQqbVKjnTKeBU_Jfo9iwsccHeIJPGaMG/Vtk1OPyVoiuRdbkeB4tPGnAQGU6tmf02/7)**...you’re helping us fund ads, appearances, events, fliers, door knockers, EVERYTHING that blankets California with the message: _You don’t have to accept failed Democrat policies that make all of us less safe, less prosperous, and less free.__**We rely on everyday Americans like you**_ — _Not liberal Hollywood elites_ —to fund this campaign. **[That’s why I’m asking you to chip in to my campaign and give us the resources we need to go up against the Kamala Harris fundraising goliath.](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/dXb1hwbrFMKi2lN5JKsB1Nqk4smrna-ZFUBJMsGLnzLV9XWyeUOcbhQ6e58FYPMkomn2DtETAP2dxnGY7OwdSRv0cwZlOyLT27l4IpM0DPtBxZqNS3FD5uD0INDY3m8ReM52VD2-nph0r4IZ0zL66eDXF97svoj8zJlbBc8z2FLgZbPtUz2P8nRF3gpjBOG42FD-UtBE4Y_uIyE6xnchnPIma8PjtvP31MlWCfcaojncl2krgpwcNhKhf8QB5wAHp6dITuHIgKk6PmwY0Bi9BroEHXqqHHmEl-jH2V-u93Zt76VaJOiHeeHrB9Ao-IH3gXQtjxyk9ugNJHNji5c6ZNAxRzH1WcLRbK9SXkerWr8QWhEo4wsbPKF-4-rv18gaM2DBaSQTRuzBLQZwgvWM_Oe884-8J7Jbzi4Z2CVn67Yj1vDKhKkHm2IXQhZYO2EihCMy-yIxNF-e8-IdnjOnRgyZMakmrto_MwT1tHacUVmOR3pXpf74vyTaPKzQ3ZM2/ibSIaJtR-aoHKBCb7i0E41foR5MLnWDE/7)** The Democrat machine that raised a billion dollars for Kamala Harris is going to come out in full force in her home state of California. But people are fed up with her, with her incompetence, and with the years of Democrat rule.Even if she outraises me 10-to-1, I’ll have a good fighting chance.This race is the toughest in the nation. But I have been doing something just as tough—tackling crime in California.Here is what Democrats don’t understand about the crime problem: when someone breaks the law or is a violent repeat offender, you put them in jail. It’s not a systemic or societal problem. Or a racism problem. It is an accountability problem.I’ve been wrangling criminals without help from these Democrats for years.And despite Liberal politicians and the local media trying to get rid of me, I’ve won two terms as Sheriff.That’s how you can know I’m the man for the job.Now I’m ready to do the hardest thing in American politics –- win statewide in California as a Republican.When we win here... defeating Kamala Harris for the second time......we become the line of defense against these failed liberal policies spreading to the rest of the country.And we stop Kamala Harris, or whoever the Democrats put up for Governor, from using California as a liberal launchpad for their extremist agenda.It all depends on you!

What should be next on our agenda for the first 100 days?
| Peter,
---
My father has already issued hundreds of executive orders to get America back on track.
---
****From securing our border to ending “woke” DEI programs in the federal government to resuming drilling to lower energy costs, he’s ENDING Biden’s insanity and putting the American people first!****
---
And this is just the beginning of restoring America’s greatness! There’s still a lot more we can accomplish during his first 100 days in office.
---
But like I’ve said before, the America First movement is built on listening to the grassroots Americans who make this country great.
---
YOU were the real power behind our historic victory in November, and we’ll never forget it.
---
_Which is why I’m going directly to our biggest grassroots supporters to ask what else you’d like my father (and our Republican majorities in Congress) to do during his first 100 days in office._
---

Which is why I’m going directly to our biggest grassroots supporters to ask what else you’d like my father (and our Republican majorities in Congress) to do during his first 100 days in office. “First 100 Days Survey” Participant: dpwillis67@gmail.com Survey ID #: 8557-154 Status: INCOMPLETE** | Thank you, Donald Trump Jr. Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC. www.elisestefanik.com PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801 |

body:
| [ ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105204299/537690544/240069735?refcode=EM_FL_OPA_20250404_fr_results_team_oc_act_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFkMTFmNWNjLWIyMTEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=PQT02WoRLA7mar8A4z0b0wwU2bIWRm-YAknJ239MmU8=)
---
**Some good news, people are showing out!**
* Despite Elon Musk spending $25 million and personally appearing in Wisconsin to interfere in the state election, his candidate lost. The people elected Judge Susan Crawford to the state supreme court, a champion with a history of defending unions and abortion rights.

* Trump-endorsed candidates in Florida’s special election, significantly underperformed predictions. This is another litmus test that voters across the spectrum do not want a president that shreds the constitution and makes huge cuts to critical federal programs.

**What does this mean and what comes next?**
* These election results show that nationwide we are fighting back and we can win in 2026 and onwards. Even when MAGA-aligned billionaires like Musk and Jeffrey Yass get involved, if we stay focused on our organizing, we win. _**[We are building power for our people: at the polls, in the streets, online, everywhere. That work requires resources, please consider a $3 gift, even that amount will go a long way towards our next big victories.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105204300/537690545/240069735?refcode=EM_FL_OPA_20250404_fr_results_team_oc_act_xx_l1&amount=3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFkMTFmNWNjLWIyMTEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=PQT02WoRLA7mar8A4z0b0wwU2bIWRm-YAknJ239MmU8=)**_

* This Saturday, tens of thousands are planning to take to the streets for the Hands Off! National Day of Action. Trump and Musk think this country belongs to them. They’re grabbing everything they can. We’re saying “keep your hands off” with hundreds of rallies hosted all across the country. You won’t want to miss this.

RSVP to join us in _**[Philadelphia (click here)](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105204301/537690546/-1389798687?refcode=EM_FL_OPA_20250404_fr_results_team_oc_act_xx_l2&ms=EM_FL_OPA_20250404_fr_results_team_oc_act_xx_l2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFkMTFmNWNjLWIyMTEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=PQT02WoRLA7mar8A4z0b0wwU2bIWRm-YAknJ239MmU8=&emci=bcd1ef7d-7211-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ad11f5cc-b211-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=3825287)**_ or _**[Pittsburgh (click here)](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105204302/537690547/-1020954392?refcode=EM_FL_OPA_20250404_fr_results_team_oc_act_xx_l3&ms=EM_FL_OPA_20250404_fr_results_team_oc_act_xx_l3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFkMTFmNWNjLWIyMTEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=PQT02WoRLA7mar8A4z0b0wwU2bIWRm-YAknJ239MmU8=&emci=bcd1ef7d-7211-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ad11f5cc-b211-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=3825287).**_

Let’s do this,

name: State Representative Ryan Chamberlin
subject: Newsletter from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin
body:
Newsletter from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/repryanchamberlin/newsletter-from-rep-ryan-chamberlin-12927319?e=d1c1b9864f)
---
| |
---
| | **NEWSLETTER FROM REP. RYAN CHAMBERLIN**
---
| Friends and Neighbors,

We had another successful week in Tallahassee! My fellow House members and I are working hard to keep our state leading the nation with **forward-thinking policy ideas** and **real solutions** to address the challenges facing our state. Throughout the week, I had the opportunity to meet with constituents and stakeholders and present my legislation in committee. It is hard to believe that we are over halfway through the **2025 Legislative Session**! We’ve accomplished so much already, but we still have a lot more to do in the remaining weeks.

**I look forward to keeping you updated on our progress. Stay tuned!**
---
| As always, my office is here to serve you! Please do not hesitate to [**contact my team**](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=00f0d775de&e=d1c1b9864f) if you need assistance with anything. We would be happy to help in any way we can!
---
| | **Committee Meetings: Week 5**
---
| Along with meeting with constituents and stakeholders, the last few days were filled with several committee meetings. Click below to read the meeting packets from each committee meeting to learn more about the bills we heard.

**Week 5 Committee Meetings:**
* [Economic Infrastructure Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=b54ce84898&e=d1c1b9864f)
* [Intergovernmental Affairs Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=0e76ac504a&e=d1c1b9864f)
* [Human Services Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=6649807916&e=d1c1b9864f)
* [Justice Budget Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=ca009c8220&e=d1c1b9864f)
* [Ways and Means Committee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=9c44821c33&e=d1c1b9864f)

---
| | **Week 5: Update on My Legislation**
---
|
---
| The laboratory industry in Florida is experiencing a critical and ongoing shortage of qualified personnel. This week, I had the opportunity to present my bill, [**HB 115 – Clinical Laboratory Personnel**](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=f57847f6f5&e=d1c1b9864f), in the **Health Professions & Programs Subcommittee**, which will work to address this shortage. I’m proud to report that it passed favorably with a committee substitute!
---
| | **Meetings in Tallahassee**
---
|
---
| My favorite Florida sheriff, **Billy Woods** , from the **Marion County Sheriff’s Office** , stopped by while he was in town for **Sheriffs Day** at the Capitol. It is always great to connect and discuss the important work being done to keep residents safe in Marion County!
---
|
---
| I had the opportunity to meet with **Sheriff Hardwick** ’s team from **St. Johns County**. They shared with me how they plan to support the children in their area. I truly appreciate their dedication to making a difference in young lives!
---
|
---
| **Taylor Cheeseman** , **Vice President of the Children’s Home Network** , stopped by for a productive conversation about the challenges their organization faces in the child welfare system. We also discussed the vital services they provide to children and families across Florida.
---
|
---
| As many of you know, this legislative session, I’m sponsoring [**HB 541 – Minimum Wage Requirements**](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=92f253054f&e=d1c1b9864f), which impacts apprenticeships. I had a valuable discussion with **Director Sean Donnelly of the Electrical Training Alliance of Central Florida** , along with two apprentices, to hear their perspectives on the bill.
---
|
---
| I always appreciate a visit from **Teach Florida**! This incredible organization is dedicated to advocating for Jewish day schools and securing enhanced security funding for these institutions. Their commitment to education and safety is truly commendable!
---
|
---
| My door is always open to **Jenny Martinez** , the **Executive Director of Marion Senior Services**. Their organization does wonderful work to support the elderly in our community, and I’m grateful for all that they do.
---
| | **In the District**
---
|
---
| Back in the district, my staff attended the **AdventHealth Heathbrook ER Groundbreaking** , which will help to expand our community’s access to healthcare.
---
|
---
| They also attended **Beautiful Belleview** , where they met city leadership and networked with local business leaders. I appreciate my team’s hard work in the district while I’m in Tallahassee for session!
---
| | **Receptions in Tallahassee**
---
| My staff and I attended various receptions and events in Tallahassee this week, including:
* **Women in Retail**
* **2025 Retail Days Reception**
* **Oceans Day Legislative Reception**
* **FL Military and Veterans Reception**
* **Florida Oceans Complimentary Fish Fry**
* **The Able Trust (High school Tech Students) Reception**
* **Legislative Breakfast - Moms for Liberty**

---
|
---
| | Thank you for reading, and I look forward to sharing with you again soon.

Have a great weekend!

Best regards,

**Ryan Chamberlin**
******State Representative**
**District 24**
---
| | _Copyright © 2024 Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative, All rights reserved._

Paid by Ryan Chamberlin, Republican, for State Representative

**Our mailing address is:**
Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative
526 E Park Ave
Tallahassee, FL 32301-2568

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/profile?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=929c76fdea&e=d1c1b9864f&c=12bc52046b) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=929c76fdea&t=b&e=d1c1b9864f&c=12bc52046b).

---

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=19456%2E878179%2EoMF66d). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=19456%2E878179%2EoMF66d).**

name: Team Haaland
subject: We’ll never take your support for granted
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Grassroots supporters like you are a critical part of how we’re building the campaign that it’s going to take to elect Deb.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=1144%2E400544%2ECg6InH)
Deb's visited communities all over the state since launching her campaign to be the next Governor of New Mexico, and the energy at each stop has been _incredible_.
**At Team Haaland, we’ll never take that support for granted.** There are some campaigns out there who treat their email list like an ATM, with no real thought for the people on the other end — but that will _never_ be us.
**With our first big reporting deadline coming up on Monday, grassroots supporters like you are a critical part of how we plan to meet our $250,000 stretch goal,[and we will need to ask you to pitch in to help us do it.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250404_2_atm?amount=5&refcode=em20250404-1144&refcode2=1144_400544_Cg6InH&akid=1144%2E400544%2ECg6InH)**
But under no circumstances will we EVER engage in some of the scammy, “how-is-this-not-illegal” tactics you might’ve seen elsewhere:
_**WARNING: FINAL NOTICE ENCLOSED**_
_**QUADRUPLE MATCH ACTIVE!**_
_**DONATE NOW OR ELSE**_
**🤢 🤢🤢**
Deb’s running for governor to make life easier for our families and make the future brighter for our kids. **Tricking you into donating won’t help accomplish that, but building a grassroots movement of folks who pitch in $5, $25, or whatever they can, will.**
So if you’re ready to elect a governor who will lower costs, make rent and housing affordable, strengthen our schools, and prevent crimes so you feel safe raising a family in New Mexico, then Deb’s counting on your help.
[**With just three days left until our big reporting deadline on Monday, will you pitch in $5 to Deb’s campaign today?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250404_2_atm?amount=5&refcode=em20250404-1144&refcode2=1144_400544_Cg6InH&akid=1144%2E400544%2ECg6InH)
We’re counting on your help to reach our $250,000 stretch goal, and we truly appreciate every dollar you donate!
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250404_2_atm?amount=5&refcode=em20250404-1144&refcode2=1144_400544_Cg6InH&akid=1144%2E400544%2ECg6InH)**
Thank you again for all the support!
Team Haaland HQ
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250404-2&t=2&refcode2=1144_400544_Cg6InH&akid=1144%2E400544%2ECg6InH)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=1144%2E400544%2ECg6InH)

clean_body: Grassroots supporters like you are a critical part of how we’re building the campaign that it’s going to take to elect Deb. Deb's visited communities all over the state since launching her campaign to be the next Governor of New Mexico, and the energy at each stop has been incredible. At Team Haaland, we’ll never take that support for granted. There are some campaigns out there who treat their email list like an ATM, with no real thought for the people on the other end — but that will never be us. With our first big reporting deadline coming up on Monday, grassroots supporters like you are a critical part of how we plan to meet our $250,000 stretch goal, But under no circumstances will we EVER engage in some of the scammy, “how-is-this-not-illegal” tactics you might’ve seen elsewhere: WARNING: FINAL NOTICE ENCLOSED QUADRUPLE MATCH ACTIVE! DONATE NOW OR ELSE 🤢 🤢🤢 Deb’s running for governor to make life easier for our families and make the future brighter for our kids. Tricking you into donating won’t help accomplish that, but building a grassroots movement of folks who pitch in $5, $25, or whatever they can, will. So if you’re ready to elect a governor who will lower costs, make rent and housing affordable, strengthen our schools, and prevent crimes so you feel safe raising a family in New Mexico, then Deb’s counting on your help. We’re counting on your help to reach our $250,000 stretch goal, and we truly appreciate every dollar you donate! Thank you again for all the support! Team Haaland HQ

name: Cory Booker
subject: I’m tired and a little hoarse, but I cannot stay silent.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
This is a moment where we must all speak up.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105204866/537708597/736086420?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-04-04_2&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZWQzMTUwZmUtYjcxMS1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=QpH09VZ_lc120RCvNAGExd69bOEPm-DQtdk-X1H_juM=)
---
Peter, it’s Cory.

This week, I stepped off the Senate floor after speaking for over 25 hours straight — because I cannot stand idly by while Donald Trump and Elon Musk completely disregard the rule of law, the Constitution, and the needs of the American people.

I may be tired and a little hoarse, but as I said again and again in the Senate: this is a moment where we cannot afford to be silent. We must speak up.

What’s clear to me is that this is just the beginning. Americans across this country, no matter their title or party, are ready to be heard.

I believe that history will show we rose to meet this moment.

It will show that we did not let the chaos and division go unanswered.

It will show that when our president chose to spread lies and sow fear, we chose to come together, to work together, and to rise together.

We need to elect Democrats up and down the ballot to counter the Trump-Musk agenda and the millions GOP groups are preparing to spend.

**First,** Donald Trump launched a war on public media, **openly stating he would “love to” defund NPR and PBS.**

**Then,** House Republicans launched the “House Oversight Subcommittee on Delivering on Government Efficiency” led by Marjorie Taylor Greene–a companion to Elon Musk’s DOGE.

**Now,** they're investigating NPR and PBS in an attempt to**defund all public media.**

As Trump and his MAGA allies, including Musk and Rep. Greene, shamefully attempt to strip Americans of access to vital journalism, we must unite to protect the integrity of public media.

name: Tom Fitton
subject: 5 Million Names Cleaned from Voter Rolls!
body:
Trump Cuts Illegal Alien Subsidies!

[INSIDE JW]

JUDICIAL WATCH VICTORY: FIVE MILLION NAMES REMOVED FROM VOTER ROLLS
NATIONWIDE

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34915&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj10UGFuUEpxaHJqVSZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=76263d08ab53cb3278a7f38d7e749abc18d04d799b521645f4ffedae8af5ce65]

A major milestone in our work to clean up voter rolls!

Over the last several years, our analysis and use of voter
registration lists have led to lawsuits and legal actions under
the National Voter Registration Act
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=435&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3YvY3J0L25hdGlvbmFsLXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi1hY3QtMTk5My1udnJhP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=f158da4a5ae1de35e565a6a8f53da89cd8322253d93d918bf210c72ca3be414b]
(NVRA) that have resulted in the removal of five million
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=21208&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL252cmEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6895639061ab0e9dd273a0f81e79d0c7c0d5d5fea1149f126ef741e00ac8c21d]
names from voter rolls in
nearly a dozen states and localities.

Recently, the Commonwealth of Kentucky reported that 735,000
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34903&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtYWRhbXMta3ktbnZyYS1jb21wbGlhbmNlLW5vdGljZS0wMDA5NC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=bc0d19050231e9e2e7b38d34efcd9fa4ab74a2999b47431616febf3c8d30105d]
ineligible voter registrations have been removed from its voter rolls
since 2019 by the State Board of Elections as part of its 2018 consent
decree settling a lawsuit with us.

As part of its 2022 settlement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=28646&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctYW5kLW55Yy1zZXR0bGUtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=f85eb002f4b9dfa8334e25686194139c3301a613cd8d8d197cbaf3272fc1aa08],
New York
City alone has removed 918,139 ineligible names from its rolls: recent
data show 477,056
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34904&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdmFsZW50aW5lLW55Yy1udnJhLXJlbW92YWxzLTIwMjMtMjAyNS0wMzk1Mi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=e75255b60e0d9142650fb9fd77ab73add57a5816b3083f563ddb2f3e405036e4]
removals between March 2023 and February 2025, which is in addition to
the 441,083 previously reported removals.

These latest developments come as a result of our 2017 lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=21656&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvanVkaWNpYWwtd2F0Y2gtc3Vlcy1rZW50dWNreS1kaXJ0eS12b3Rlci1yZWdpc3RyYXRpb24tcm9sbHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6776882e5ffef9002cf55a382fd8a713db0f7413afb807cddda40abf525544fa]
against Kentucky under the NVRA (_Judicial Watch, Inc. and the United
States of America v. Alison Lundergan Grimes, et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=4373&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnQtYXJjaGl2ZS9qdy12LWdyaW1lcy1ldC1hbC1rZW50dWNreS1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDAwOTQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=858eae13fc9682040f63f3ff6799ee4953855e74e8dcdcefa8137e5f8e43dd17]
(No. 3:17-cv-00094)) and our 2022 lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=27650&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbnljLXZvdGVyLXJvbGxzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=f694f98b6c603564cb078b53396ef8afb591ec9c885af0ba34a5665de69319ca]
against New York City
that pointed out that it had removed only 22 names under the federal
law over six years (_Judicial Watch v Valentine et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=27639&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL255LWJvYXJkLW9mLWVsZWN0aW9ucy1udnJhLWp1bHktMjAyMi1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDM5NTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=ae6f2d4f9d6f18974428344524a75725affc520a7b1e0efa7cc0420c1daad9c3]
(No.1:22-cv-03952)).

> mortgages through a Federal Housing Administration (FHA) program
> that lends to “non-permanent residents.” The new policy ensures
> that illegal aliens cannot access FHA-insured financing and
> refocuses the use of taxpayer-funded federal housing programs to
> benefit U.S. citizens.

> DHS Secretary Noem points out that the Biden administration
> prioritized illegal aliens over American citizens, including by
> giving them taxpayer-funded housing. “Not anymore,” she says in
> the press release
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34920&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaHVkLmdvdi9uZXdzL2h1ZC1uby0yNS0wNDY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=37a75c0e9f6d13fe0a31517d99ab755d8b8bb8bfc82a4a7f04f57c190d249ab9]
> announcing the new HUD partnership. “The entire government will
> work together to identify abuse and exploitation of public benefits
> and make sure those in this country illegally are not receiving
> federal benefits or other financial incentives to stay illegally.”
> The DHS Secretary further warns that the gravy train is over for
> those in the country illegally. “If you are an illegal immigrant,
> you should leave now,” Noem said. HUD Secretary Turner assures his
> agency’s new agreement with DHS will leverage resources to ensure
> American people are the only priority when it comes to public
> housing.

> Uncle Sam spends a whopping $42 billion to provide illegal
> immigrants with an array of welfare services, according to CIS
> figures presented to Congress less than a year ago. This includes
> housing, food, healthcare, public education, and other social
> services. As an example of the toll this is taking on local
> communities, CIS revealed that the city of New York has spent or
> expects to spend $12 billion over the next three years on housing,
> food, health care, and other services for recently arrived illegal
> immigrants. Chicago spent around $361 million to accommodate
> recently arrived migrants, the District of Columbia about $36.4
> million and Denver approximately $180 million, more than triple what
> it spends on the homeless. A few years ago, Judicial Watch reported
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=30724&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY29zdC1vZi1pbGxlZ2FsLWltbWlncmF0aW9uLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=932376b0ede1e4556707ed98a363911ea33a09c41fdd7f6a1ddb7c76b72b411b]
on a
> congressional probe that determined the cost of illegal immigration
> is greater than the annual gross domestic product of 15 states.

D.C. ANTI-CRIME: EX-CON HIRED AS VIOLENCE INTERRUPTER CHARGED WITH
MURDER

The local DC government can be a dangerous mess. Consider
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34895&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZGMtYW50aS1jcmltZS1wcm9ncmFtLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=83a87225adacd0998d8cebdaacbbea3c70036c20e940fdb0827b1a044477493a]
its attempts to
fight crime. From our _Corruption Chronicles _blog:

> The scandals keep piling up in the District of Columbia’s
> ill-fated program to curb violence with an ex-con charged with two
> felonies since D.C. officials hired him to be a violence
> interrupter, a disgraced councilman—Trayon White
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34921&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3YvdXNhby1kYy9wci9kYy1jaXR5LWNvdW5jaWxtZW1iZXItYXJyZXN0ZWQtYnJpYmVyeS1jaGFyZ2Vdc+KAlGJ1c3RlZD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=2d89e53dd7f4ea15596df95480ba42a6153df155055e9ec18018c136af3706d8
> for taking tens of thousands of dollars in cash bribes to help
> extend violence prevention and youth services contracts and another
> high-ranking official pleading guilty to bribery
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34922&F=T&&hash=f311a65b4b8948770a1c12bf594fd9de438ed2fd43cf0bb97f1f98788a01943a]
> for using her official government position to help a friend get
> contracts and grants. As millions of taxpayer dollars pour into
> D.C.’s questionable anti-violence initiative, fraud and corruption
> continue to rock the program, and crime remains high in the
> neighborhoods it is supposed to help.

> The repeat criminal works for Cure the Streets
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=21454&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9vYWcuZGMuZ292L3B1YmxpYy1zYWZldHkvY3VyZS1zdHJlZXRzLW9hZ3MtdmlvbGVuY2UtaW50ZXJydXB0aW9uLXByb2dyYW0/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=8c4315e38671df14e0b98fd9508a087734e223ad56255fd8a791b38f31983945],
> a public safety program launched by former D.C. Attorney General
> Karl Racine to reduce gun violence by treating it as a disease that
> can be interrupted and stopped from spreading. Cure the Streets
> typically hires people with criminal histories as violence
> interrupters because they know first-hand about the challenges that
> residents of crime-infested communities live with. Racine, a
> two-term D.C. Attorney General who is currently a partner in a major
> corporate law firm
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34923&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaG9nYW5sb3ZlbGxzLmNvbS9lbi9yYWNpbmUta2FybD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=d8d4a3b2480ee27c3119dd2adced18b06131920e95738e65636aeb90f68db773],
> claimed the “transformed criminals” hired by his program perform
> community-driven public safety work that can avoid using police by
> interrupting potentially violent conflicts because they have
> relationships and influence within targeted neighborhoods. The
> program operates in notoriously high-crime sections throughout D.C.,
> which are broken down by wards, including Eckington/Truxton and
> Trinidad in Ward 5, Marshall Heights/Benning Heights in Ward 7 and
> Bellevue, Washington Highlands, and Congress Heights in Ward 8.

> A Cure the Streets employee, Cotey Wynn, was recently arrested and
> faces a first-degree murder charge
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34924&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9tcGRjLmRjLmdvdi9yZWxlYXNlL21wZC1tYWtlcy10d28tYXJyZXN0cy0yMDIzLW5pZ2h0Y2x1Yi1ob21pY2lkZV1yZWxhdGVkP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=20e79426ef807c5576d588158e37e380867bf2300ef8ca991035ae54ca3a8fa5
> to a nightclub shooting in which a 31-year-old former college
> basketball player was killed. Wynn has an extensive rap sheet and
> had served ten years in prison when Racine, D.C.’s then chief
> legal officer, hired him as a violence interrupter. His record
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34925&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9tcGRjLmRjLmdvdi9yZWxlYXNlL2FycmVzdC1tYWRlLTIwMTctaG9taWNpZGUtOTAwLWJsb2NrLTEydGgtc3RyZWV0LW5vcnRoZWFzdF1pbmNsdWRlcz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=e1b1428cb8b25d1c2030ecb4f49839d9ff2043f6e550db75b6cbb424e4763c93
> felony murder, first degree murder, possession with intent to
> distribute crack cocaine, and distribution of a controlled
> substance, according to the Metropolitan Police Department. The most
> recent criminal charge is not the first since becoming a D.C.
> violence interrupter. In 2020 Judicial Watch reported
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=34926&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZXgtY29uLWhpcmVkLWJ5LWQtYy1hcy12aW9sZW5jZS1pbnRlcnJ1cHRlci1hcnJlc3RlZC1jaGFyZ2VkLXdpdGgtbXVyZGVyL110aGF0P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=50ccb3cf5bef2031dac83aa2b01e4f7b0dbe176257c23ade502cd3e3a443167c
> Wynn got arrested and charged for fatally shooting a 53-year-old man
> in 2017 near the Trinidad neighborhood in Northeast Washington. At
> the time of that arrest Wynn was under the supervision of the
> Pretrial Services Agency for the District of Columbia, a federal
> agency that believes preventative detention should only be a last
> resort for defendants,
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73214&L=21457&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucHNhLmdvdi8/cT1hYm91dC9taXNzaW9uX3Zpc2lvbiZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=849d0042381cb88c599bf12aa925b4cdeba0f4683b3c76ac6d0a2abea7284898]
> who should live in the least restrictive conditions while awaiting
> court.

> The public officials embroiled in bribery scandals are part of the
> D.C. Office of Neighborhood Safety and Engagement (ONSE), the
> umbrella agency that more broadly focuses on reducing violence in
> the nation’s crime-infested capitol area. White, recently expelled
> by the D.C. Council and scheduled to be tried in 2026, took $156,000
> in cash payments in exchange for using his position as a D.C.
> councilman to pressure government employees at ONSE and the
> Department of Youth Rehabilitation Services (DYRS) to extend several
> contracts. “The contracts at issue were valued at $5.2 million and
> were for two companies to provide Violence Intervention services in
> D.C.,” according to the Department of Justice (DOJ), which reveals
> that the disgraced lawmaker took payments of $35,000 in cash on four
> separate occasions. Just a few days ago, the agency disclosed the
> most recent ONSE official nabbed in a bribery scheme, a former
> deputy director named Dana McDaniel who has pleaded guilty to
> accepting at least $10,000 in exchange for using her position to
> award contracts and grants to businesses owned by a Maryland-based
> associate. As ONSE deputy director, McDaniel managed agency
> programming and community-based services focused on providing
> resources and interventions for at-risk individuals in at-risk
> communities impacted by violence in D.C. She faces 15 years in
> prison.

Until next week,

clean_body: Trump Cuts Illegal Alien Subsidies! [INSIDE JW] JUDICIAL WATCH VICTORY: FIVE MILLION NAMES REMOVED FROM VOTER ROLLS NATIONWIDE [] A major milestone in our work to clean up voter rolls! Over the last several years, our analysis and use of voter registration lists have led to lawsuits and legal actions under the National Voter Registration Act [] (NVRA) that have resulted in the removal of five million [] names from voter rolls in nearly a dozen states and localities. Recently, the Commonwealth of Kentucky reported that 735,000 [] ineligible voter registrations have been removed from its voter rolls since 2019 by the State Board of Elections as part of its 2018 consent decree settling a lawsuit with us. As part of its 2022 settlement [], New York City alone has removed 918,139 ineligible names from its rolls: recent data show 477,056 [] removals between March 2023 and February 2025, which is in addition to the 441,083 previously reported removals. These latest developments come as a result of our 2017 lawsuit [] against Kentucky under the NVRA (Judicial Watch, Inc. and the United States of America v. Alison Lundergan Grimes, et al. [] (No. 3:17-cv-00094)) and our 2022 lawsuit [] against New York City that pointed out that it had removed only 22 names under the federal law over six years (Judicial Watch v Valentine et al. [] (No.1:22-cv-03952)). In 2018, the U.S. Supreme Court confirmed [] that such removals are mandatory. In May 2022, Los Angeles County [] confirmed the removal of 1.2 million [] ineligible names from its rolls as part of a settlement [] in a federal lawsuit [] we filed in 2017. (Legal pressure from Judicial Watch ultimately has led to the removal of ineligible names [] from voter rolls in New York, California, Pennsylvania, Colorado, North Carolina, Kentucky, and Ohio.) Other voter roll clean-up lawsuits by Judicial Watch continue in Illinois, Oregon, and California. Our clean-up of over five million dirty names from voter rolls is a historic achievement for clean elections. I have no doubt that our election integrity heavy lifting helped stop the steal in 2024. But there are millions of more names to be removed from voting rolls, which is why we are in federal court in three states. We are a national leader [] in voting integrity and voting rights. Recently, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit declined to rehear [] its previous ruling in which it agreed with us that it was unlawful for Mississippi to count ballots that arrived after Election Day. In March 2025 we filed a federal lawsuit [] against California on behalf of U.S. Rep. Darrell Issa to prevent state election officials from extending Election Day for seven days beyond the date established by federal law. California counts ballots received up to seven days after Election Day. Also in March, we sent a notice letter [] to Lt. Governor Deidre M. Henderson, notifying her that Utah is currently in violation of the NVRA’s public disclosure requirements. The notice letter warns of a lawsuit after 90 days if the issues are not resolved. JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR INFO ON BIDEN GRANTS TO LEFTISTS There’s no end to the malicious use of your tax dollars by the far-left bureaucrats working at the direction of Joe Biden. We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] against the U.S. Department of State for records on grants made by the Biden State Department to various Hungarian media outlets and educational organizations opposing conservative Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of State [] (No. 1:25-cv-00836)). Judicial Watch sued after the State Department failed to respond to a January 29, 2025, FOIA request for: All records regarding any grants provided under the Independent Media Support Fund/Free Media Grant Program to any organization or individual in Hungary. This request includes, but is not limited to, all records depicting the recipients and purposes of the grants, the timing of the awards, and all related records of communication between former Ambassador David Pressman or Chargé d’Affaires David Holmes and any other individual or entity. The complaint explains: According to media reports, on the final day of the Biden Administration, the U.S. Embassy in Budapest, Hungary, awarded grants worth millions of Hungarian Forints to various Hungarian media outlets and educational organizations aligned with opponents of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, an ally of incoming U.S. President Donald Trump. See, e.g., “U.S. Embassy Awards Millions in Grants to Opposition Media on Trump Inauguration Day,” Hungarian Conservative, Jan. 21, 2025 (available at ) According to the Hungarian Conservative: Recipients of the grant include several well-known anti-government media outlets, aligned with the Hungarian opposition but posing as independent press. Among the grantees, 444 received more than 8 million HUF (over 20,000 USD), Átlátszó 10 million HUF approximately 25,000 USD), Jelen 7 million HUF (around 18,000 USD), and both Magyar Hang and Klubrádió were awarded 10 million HUF each in Category A. In Category B, which supports media education and journalism training, Transparency International’s Hungarian foundation—one of the most prominent organizations within the Soros network—received more than 7 million HUF. The Biden State Department, on Joe’s last day in office, apparently sent a cash infusion to Hungary’s left-wing, anti-Orban opposition organizations. Let’s hope the new State Department stops the cover-up and produces records on this meddling. In February 2025, we also sued [] USAID for records regarding waste, fraud and abuse tied to aid money sent to Ukraine. In November 2024, we sued [] the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) for records about to the $27 million in U.S. grants awarded to “Miscellaneous Foreign Awardees” that have been designated for use in Gaza. In October 2023, we sued [] the State Department for communications and records regarding the eligibility of citizens of Hungary to participate in a visa waiver program. In 2018, we obtained State Department documents [] showing top Soros representatives in Romania collaborating with the State Department in a program jointly funded by [], among others, Soros’s Open Society Foundations – Romania [] and USAID, called the “Open Government Partnership [ Additional State Department records [] uncovered in 2018 showed USAID funding for George Soros’s left-wing nonprofit organizations in Albania. The documents dealt primarily with the activities of Soros’ top operative in Albania, Andri Dobrushi, the director of Open Society Foundation-Albania, who was actively engaged in channeling funding to what Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban calls Soros’ “mercenary army.” The documents showed U.S. grant money flowing through non-governmental organizations (NGOs) that profess to promote “civil society,” while in fact attacking traditional, pro-American groups, governments and policies. Other records [] related to the USAID’s activities in Albania showed that the Obama administration sent U.S. taxpayers’ funds to a group backed by Soros, which used the money to fund left-wing political activities in Albania, including working with the country’s socialist government to push for highly controversial judicial “reform.” The records also detailed how the Soros operation helped the State Department review grant applications from other groups for taxpayer funding. ‘THE GRAVY TRAIN IS OVER’—HUD, DHS END SUBSIDIZED HOUSING FOR ILLEGALS The Trump administration is creating a process to save taxpayers billions of dollars given to illegal aliens by the Biden administration. Our Corruption Chronicles blog reports []. Over half of illegal immigrant households in the United States use at least one taxpayer-funded welfare program, according to congressional testimony [] provided recently by the Center for Immigration Studies (CIS), and it costs the government tens of billions of dollars. Clamping down on the unprecedented flow of migrants is only the first step to mitigate the damage of the Biden administration’s disastrous open border policies. One major area of concern is government-subsidized public housing, which became available to illegal aliens under policies established by the previous administration’s Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD). To reverse the damage amid a nationwide affordable housing crisis, two key government agencies have teamed up to crack down on illegal immigrants benefiting from taxpayer-funded public housing. Trump HUD Secretary Scott Turner and Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem recently signed a Memorandum of Understanding [] (MOU) to end the wasteful misappropriation of taxpayer dollars to benefit illegal aliens instead of American citizens, especially veterans in need. Under the agreement HUD will provide a full-time staff member to assist in operations at the Incident Command Center (ICC), creating an interagency partnership to facilitate data sharing and ensure taxpayer-funded housing programs are not used to harbor or benefit illegal aliens. “We will safeguard precious taxpayer resources, and we will stop the harmful practices of the former Biden Administration, which favored and prioritized illegal aliens over American citizens in the expenditure of housing funds and subsidies,” the HUD-DHS MOU states. The agencies will collaborate to facilitate and coordinate efforts to place Americans first by establishing lines of communications and developing joint measures to accomplish President Trump’s directive to protect Americans and their resources. “Further, we will work together to identify criminal aliens who prey upon and victimize the American people,” the MOU reads. “By working more closely together and with greater collaboration, we will identify illegal aliens who are ineligible for Federal housing assistance and take remedial measures to end this waste and abuse, including referral for immigration enforcement actions.” A few days later HUD also announced [ illegal immigrants would no longer qualify for government-backed mortgages through a Federal Housing Administration (FHA) program that lends to “non-permanent residents.” The new policy ensures that illegal aliens cannot access FHA-insured financing and refocuses the use of taxpayer-funded federal housing programs to benefit U.S. citizens. DHS Secretary Noem points out that the Biden administration prioritized illegal aliens over American citizens, including by giving them taxpayer-funded housing. “Not anymore,” she says in the press release [] announcing the new HUD partnership. “The entire government will work together to identify abuse and exploitation of public benefits and make sure those in this country illegally are not receiving federal benefits or other financial incentives to stay illegally.” The DHS Secretary further warns that the gravy train is over for those in the country illegally. “If you are an illegal immigrant, you should leave now,” Noem said. HUD Secretary Turner assures his agency’s new agreement with DHS will leverage resources to ensure American people are the only priority when it comes to public housing. Uncle Sam spends a whopping $42 billion to provide illegal immigrants with an array of welfare services, according to CIS figures presented to Congress less than a year ago. This includes housing, food, healthcare, public education, and other social services. As an example of the toll this is taking on local communities, CIS revealed that the city of New York has spent or expects to spend $12 billion over the next three years on housing, food, health care, and other services for recently arrived illegal immigrants. Chicago spent around $361 million to accommodate recently arrived migrants, the District of Columbia about $36.4 million and Denver approximately $180 million, more than triple what it spends on the homeless. A few years ago, Judicial Watch reported [] on a congressional probe that determined the cost of illegal immigration is greater than the annual gross domestic product of 15 states. D.C. ANTI-CRIME: EX-CON HIRED AS VIOLENCE INTERRUPTER CHARGED WITH MURDER The local DC government can be a dangerous mess. Consider [] its attempts to fight crime. From our Corruption Chronicles blog: The scandals keep piling up in the District of Columbia’s ill-fated program to curb violence with an ex-con charged with two felonies since D.C. officials hired him to be a violence interrupter, a disgraced councilman—Trayon White [ for taking tens of thousands of dollars in cash bribes to help extend violence prevention and youth services contracts and another high-ranking official pleading guilty to bribery [] for using her official government position to help a friend get contracts and grants. As millions of taxpayer dollars pour into D.C.’s questionable anti-violence initiative, fraud and corruption continue to rock the program, and crime remains high in the neighborhoods it is supposed to help. The repeat criminal works for Cure the Streets [], a public safety program launched by former D.C. Attorney General Karl Racine to reduce gun violence by treating it as a disease that can be interrupted and stopped from spreading. Cure the Streets typically hires people with criminal histories as violence interrupters because they know first-hand about the challenges that residents of crime-infested communities live with. Racine, a two-term D.C. Attorney General who is currently a partner in a major corporate law firm [], claimed the “transformed criminals” hired by his program perform community-driven public safety work that can avoid using police by interrupting potentially violent conflicts because they have relationships and influence within targeted neighborhoods. The program operates in notoriously high-crime sections throughout D.C., which are broken down by wards, including Eckington/Truxton and Trinidad in Ward 5, Marshall Heights/Benning Heights in Ward 7 and Bellevue, Washington Highlands, and Congress Heights in Ward 8. A Cure the Streets employee, Cotey Wynn, was recently arrested and faces a first-degree murder charge [ to a nightclub shooting in which a 31-year-old former college basketball player was killed. Wynn has an extensive rap sheet and had served ten years in prison when Racine, D.C.’s then chief legal officer, hired him as a violence interrupter. His record [ felony murder, first degree murder, possession with intent to distribute crack cocaine, and distribution of a controlled substance, according to the Metropolitan Police Department. The most recent criminal charge is not the first since becoming a D.C. violence interrupter. In 2020 Judicial Watch reported [ Wynn got arrested and charged for fatally shooting a 53-year-old man in 2017 near the Trinidad neighborhood in Northeast Washington. At the time of that arrest Wynn was under the supervision of the Pretrial Services Agency for the District of Columbia, a federal agency that believes preventative detention should only be a last resort for defendants, [] who should live in the least restrictive conditions while awaiting court. The public officials embroiled in bribery scandals are part of the D.C. Office of Neighborhood Safety and Engagement (ONSE), the umbrella agency that more broadly focuses on reducing violence in the nation’s crime-infested capitol area. White, recently expelled by the D.C. Council and scheduled to be tried in 2026, took $156,000 in cash payments in exchange for using his position as a D.C. councilman to pressure government employees at ONSE and the Department of Youth Rehabilitation Services (DYRS) to extend several contracts. “The contracts at issue were valued at $5.2 million and were for two companies to provide Violence Intervention services in D.C.,” according to the Department of Justice (DOJ), which reveals that the disgraced lawmaker took payments of $35,000 in cash on four separate occasions. Just a few days ago, the agency disclosed the most recent ONSE official nabbed in a bribery scheme, a former deputy director named Dana McDaniel who has pleaded guilty to accepting at least $10,000 in exchange for using her position to award contracts and grants to businesses owned by a Maryland-based associate. As ONSE deputy director, McDaniel managed agency programming and community-based services focused on providing resources and interventions for at-risk individuals in at-risk communities impacted by violence in D.C. She faces 15 years in prison. Peter,

**Republicans just got one step closer to overturning an election.** In a Friday ruling, two right-wing judges on the North Carolina Court of Appeals sided with the losing GOP candidate in November’s state supreme court race. They sided with the failed candidate trying to toss out over 65,000 legally cast ballots in an attempt to flip the outcome of the election. It’s hard to imagine a more flagrant attack on the sanctity of our elections. [**This team helped re-elect Democratic Justice Allison Riggs in 2024’s North Carolina Supreme Court race, and now we’re continuing the fight to protect free and fair elections from the MAGA GOP. Rush a donation of $30 or more right now to help power this democracy-saving work.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105205090/537719470/1925646595?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_NCRR2_email_20250404-NDRC-Tier1-NCRR2_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250404-NDRC-Tier1-NCRR2_&utm_campaign=NCRR2&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjYyYTZhZWU4LWJhMTEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=0DyrPO3WhWqCs3FfYTBhPcNmD36AvYKqs2YWL8ASgv4=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Let’s be clear about how we got here.
Justice Riggs — one of the Democracy Defenders we were proud to endorse in 2024 — won re-election last November fair and square. Her victory was confirmed by a recount. Then it was confirmed by _another_ recount.
But the failed challenger in that election, Jefferson Griffin, has refused to concede and has instead challenged over 65,000 legitimate ballots.
That’s why Justice Riggs’ victory still has not been certified.
And while she will continue fighting in the courts to uphold the will of the people, Friday’s ruling puts North Carolina Republicans one step closer to stealing a seat on the state supreme court.
**Make no mistake: if Republicans succeed in overturning an election in North Carolina, they will export this blueprint to try to toss out legal votes everywhere they can get away with it.**
And let’s remember another big piece of the long game here: If Republicans steal this state supreme court seat, they’ll be in a stronger position to have a conservative majority on the court during the next round of post-census redistricting.
That majority could sign off on gerrymandered maps that give the GOP unearned power in the state legislature as well as Congress.
And it wouldn’t be the first time. In 2023, a new right-wing majority on the North Carolina Supreme Court overturned its _extremely_ recent precedent to greenlight a Republican map that helped flip three seats in the U.S. House of Representatives. If it weren’t for those three seats, Democrats could have won the House last November. And if Democrats had the majority right now, they could slam the brakes on Donald Trump’s agenda.
**From stealing House seats through gerrymandering, to potentially stealing a state supreme court seat by overturning an election, the MAGA GOP will continue their efforts to seize power at all costs.**
But we are fighting back: to defeat candidates who try to obstruct the will of the people, to elect Democracy Defenders like Justice Allison Riggs, to protect fair representation by ensuring a fair count in the census, to enact our 10 Year Plan for fair maps, and more.
[**If you’re outraged by the North Carolina GOP’s attempt to silence voters and reverse the outcome of an election they lost, then please pitch in a donation of $30 or more right now to help push back on the far right’s anti-democracy agenda.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105205097/537719477/1925646595?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_NCRR2_email_20250404-NDRC-Tier1-NCRR2_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250404-NDRC-Tier1-NCRR2_&utm_campaign=NCRR2&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjYyYTZhZWU4LWJhMTEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=0DyrPO3WhWqCs3FfYTBhPcNmD36AvYKqs2YWL8ASgv4=)
More soon,
Team NDRC

clean_body: It’s hard to imagine a more flagrant attack on the sanctity of our elections. | Peter, Republicans just got one step closer to overturning an election. In a Friday ruling, two right-wing judges on the North Carolina Court of Appeals sided with the losing GOP candidate in November’s state supreme court race. They sided with the failed candidate trying to toss out over 65,000 legally cast ballots in an attempt to flip the outcome of the election. It’s hard to imagine a more flagrant attack on the sanctity of our elections. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Let’s be clear about how we got here. Justice Riggs — one of the Democracy Defenders we were proud to endorse in 2024 — won re-election last November fair and square. Her victory was confirmed by a recount. Then it was confirmed by another recount. But the failed challenger in that election, Jefferson Griffin, has refused to concede and has instead challenged over 65,000 legitimate ballots. That’s why Justice Riggs’ victory still has not been certified. And while she will continue fighting in the courts to uphold the will of the people, Friday’s ruling puts North Carolina Republicans one step closer to stealing a seat on the state supreme court. Make no mistake: if Republicans succeed in overturning an election in North Carolina, they will export this blueprint to try to toss out legal votes everywhere they can get away with it. And let’s remember another big piece of the long game here: If Republicans steal this state supreme court seat, they’ll be in a stronger position to have a conservative majority on the court during the next round of post-census redistricting. That majority could sign off on gerrymandered maps that give the GOP unearned power in the state legislature as well as Congress. And it wouldn’t be the first time. In 2023, a new right-wing majority on the North Carolina Supreme Court overturned its extremely recent precedent to greenlight a Republican map that helped flip three seats in the U.S. House of Representatives. If it weren’t for those three seats, Democrats could have won the House last November. And if Democrats had the majority right now, they could slam the brakes on Donald Trump’s agenda. From stealing House seats through gerrymandering, to potentially stealing a state supreme court seat by overturning an election, the MAGA GOP will continue their efforts to seize power at all costs. But we are fighting back: to defeat candidates who try to obstruct the will of the people, to elect Democracy Defenders like Justice Allison Riggs, to protect fair representation by ensuring a fair count in the census, to enact our 10 Year Plan for fair maps, and more. = More soon, Team NDRC = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

name: Friends of Lofgren
subject: We can fight back against Musk and Trump

body:
Click here to donate to reelect Representative Zoe Lofgren >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/e-zoe-lofgren-q22025?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-04-04_MSK_RDT_ES-20070&amount=25]
Dear Friend –
Donald Trump and Elon Musk have made it their mission to wreak havoc on our country. And they will succeed, if we don’t fight back.
In just the past two weeks, Republicans and Donald Trump have passed record-high tariffs that could tank our economy, sworn to cut funding from public schools if they don’t dismantle DEI policies, and accidentally deported a United States citizen to a prison in El Salvador.
Donald Trump and Elon Musk’s actions are causing enormous damage to our nation. But, our efforts to fight back against Musk might be working—we may have forced Donald Trump to fire him.
We can resist, and we must. Here are some steps we can take to fight back and protect our democracy from Trump and Musk this week:
→ Attending in-person or virtual town halls. Check your town or county bulletin for the week, see what events are taking place, attend if you can, and keep the pressure on.
→ Call or write your local, state, and Congressional representatives (both Democratic and Republican) to make your voice and concerns heard.
→ Donating to Democrats like Rep. Zoe Lofgren who are leading the resistance against Trump in Congress.
Chip in $25 to keep Zoe in Congress, leading the resistance against Donald Trump and Elon Musk. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/e-zoe-lofgren-q22025?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-04-04_MSK_RDT_ES-20070&amount=25]
Zoe is working in Congress to lead the resistance against Trump and help elect Democratic candidates who will fight back against Republicans’ extremist agenda.
Please take action now by contributing at least $25 to help reelect Zoe and elect Democrats across California. We need strong leaders like Zoe in our government to lead the resistance against Trump and Musk. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/e-zoe-lofgren-q22025?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-04-04_MSK_RDT_ES-20070&amount=25]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your contribution will go through immediately:
EXPRESS DONATE: $5 >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/e-zoe-lofgren-q22025?expresslane=true&refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-04-04_MSK_RDT_ES-20070&amount=5]
Join us and Rep. Zoe Lofgren in the resistance.
Friends of Lofgren
Congresswoman Zoe Lofgren is a strong fighter for California’s values in Washington. As the Chair of the California Democratic Congressional Delegation, she has been on the front lines of expanding and protecting the Democratic Majority. Zoe has dedicated her career to fighting for our diverse communities and needs your help.
Click here to donate to Zoe’s campaign → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/e-zoe-lofgren-q12025?refcode=emfooter&amount=25]

San Jose, CA 95113
United States

name: Dominic Rapini
subject: Another Win for Common Sense: Wisconsin Voters Embrace Voter ID
body:
Wisconsin just delivered a powerful reminder: common sense still matters.

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us16.campaign-archive.com/?e=8763c56857&u=ebcefd906ec01db66e79c886c&id=74613acbcb)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| | | | | ““The right to vote is the crown jewel of American liberties, and we will not see its luster diminished.” ” _**President, Ronald Reagan**_
---
|
---
| **Another Win for Common Sense: Wisconsin Voters Embrace Voter ID**
---
| | | | | | | | | | [](https://dominicforct.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ebcefd906ec01db66e79c886c&id=391c3e3d45&e=8763c56857)
---
|
---
| **Opinion | Election Integrity**
**Score One for Common Sense:****Wisconsin Voters Embrace Voter ID**
**—By Dominic Rapini, American Businessman and Political Commentator**
In an era where trust in American institutions seems increasingly scarce, the voters of Wisconsin just delivered a powerful reminder: **common sense still matters**.
On Tuesday, Wisconsin citizens voted to enshrine their existing voter ID law into the state constitution, affirming—by a comfortable margin—that securing our elections with valid government-issued identification is not only prudent, but essential. With **62% support and rising** , the message from voters is crystal clear: **they want elections they can trust**.
The fact that it took a constitutional amendment to reinforce what should be a bipartisan no-brainer is, sadly, a sign of the times. Despite overwhelming national support—**poll after poll shows voter ID laws enjoying 70% to 80% approval across all demographics** —Democratic-aligned interest groups like the **ACLU** , **Common Cause** , and others scrambled to oppose this measure. Their argument? That the amendment “sets a troubling precedent.”
What’s troubling, frankly, is the persistent refusal by Democratic leaders and their legal proxies to acknowledge what the American public intuitively understands: **elections require both access and accountability**. One without the other breeds chaos—or worse, apathy. When citizens feel that their vote may be canceled out by fraud, manipulation, or poor oversight, they begin to disengage. That’s the **real threat to democracy**.
Let’s call it what it is: this is another example of the **Democratic Party choosing to fight against an 80/20 issue** —putting ideology and partisanship over the will of the people. The same groups that argue you should need an ID to pick up concert tickets or board a plane somehow believe it’s unreasonable to ask for one when electing the leaders of the free world.
Voter ID is not about exclusion. It’s about **basic election hygiene** —a fundamental layer of defense that builds faith in the process for everyone, regardless of party affiliation. The left’s insistence that it is a form of voter suppression has been eroded by time, data, and now, the will of Wisconsin voters.
To their credit, **Wisconsin Republicans** saw the writing on the wall and moved proactively to codify voter ID into the constitution, ensuring that no future legislature can sweep it away in a partisan overreach. It was a **simple act of political maturity** , supported by **former President Trump** and embraced by voters from Green Bay to Madison. It represents the kind of **blocking and tackling** we need to **restore trust in our electoral systems** —slow, deliberate, and grounded in principle.
The battle for election integrity is far from over. But Wisconsin’s vote is a **significant step in the right direction** —a quiet, decisive stand for **accountability at the ballot box**. For those of us who believe that **trust in elections is the foundation of a functioning republic** , this week’s result offers both validation and a glimmer of optimism.
Score one for common sense.**Now if only we could bring some Wisconsin common sense to Connecticut!**

* * *

---
